title: salix glauca var. lapponum
intro: Salix glauca var. lapponum, also known as the Lapland willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The medicinal properties of the Lapland willow lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat conditions such as arthritis, and it has also been used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of the Lapland willow include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
